question_types.title: Finding the Domain of a Function Algebraically
question_types.mapping_rationale: These items focus on determining the domain of functions, which most naturally aligns to interpreting valid input values for a function. Because the work is done algebraically through expression constraints in polynomial, rational, and radical forms, the task also depends substantially on analyzing expression structure.

question_types.title: Identifying Intervals of Increase and Decrease from a Graph
question_types.mapping_rationale: These questions ask students to read key behavioral features directly from graphs, specifically where a function is increasing or decreasing. That is primarily a graph-interpretation skill. Domain awareness is only a minor support here because students must express the behavior over x-intervals.

question_types.title: Calculating Average Rate of Change Algebraically
question_types.mapping_rationale: These items directly assess average rate of change over an interval. Students must evaluate function values and apply the difference quotient correctly, with algebraic structure serving as a supporting skill rather than the main target.

question_types.title: Determining Parity (Even/Odd/Neither) Algebraically
question_types.mapping_rationale: These items ask students to determine whether a function is even, odd, or neither by analyzing the effect of replacing x with -x. The task is partly about recognizing a key feature of a function and partly about manipulating algebraic structure to test symmetry. This mapping is intentionally conservative unless the overlay explicitly names parity or symmetry under a dedicated code.
